D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Doppler Effect

speed up a moving source and watch its waves compress ahead and stretch behind

A moving emitter leaves each successive wavefront behind a moving centre — front wavelengths compress, back wavelengths stretch.

Slide the source Mach number (v/c). The observer in front sees a higher frequency f', the one behind sees a lower f'. Past Mach 1 the wavefronts pile up into a cone.
